---
title: "first()"
description: "first: emit the first value matching `pred` (or simply the first value), then COMPLETE. EDGE (RxJS divergence, flagged — same class as last/find/elementAt): if the source COMPLETEs with no matching value, the substrate auto-cascades a bare COMPLETE (no value); RxJS `first()` throws EmptyError. Could align to `[[ERROR, EmptyError]]` (expressible) if strict RxJS parity is wanted."
---

<!-- generated by website/scripts/gen-api-docs.mjs; do not edit -->

first: emit the first value matching `pred` (or simply the first value), then COMPLETE. EDGE
(RxJS divergence, flagged — same class as last/find/elementAt): if the source COMPLETEs with no
matching value, the substrate auto-cascades a bare COMPLETE (no value); RxJS `first()` throws
EmptyError. Could align to `[[ERROR, EmptyError]]` (expressible) if strict RxJS parity is wanted.

## Import

```ts
import { first } from "@graphrefly/ts/operators";
```

## Signature

```ts
function first(pred?: (v: S) => boolean): Operator<S, S>
```

## Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `pred` | `(v: S) => boolean` | Missing @param pred JSDoc. |

## Returns

`Operator<S, S>`
